aiTitle: Fix ordering in Django admin related field filters

content:
-
You are a scope-aware ReAct agent. The current phase is 'atomic' at granularity 'atomic'. On every turn emit exactly one block of three lines:
  Thought: <atomic> <free-form reasoning at the atomic level>
  Action: {"name": "<tool_name>", "args": {...}}
  Confidence: <integer 0-100>

Problem: Ordering problem in admin.RelatedFieldListFilter and admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter
Description
	
RelatedFieldListFilter doesn't fall back to the ordering defined in Model._meta.ordering. 
Ordering gets set to an empty tuple in ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L196 and unless ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class it stays an empty tuple. IMHO it should fall back to the ordering defined in the related model's Meta.ordering field.
RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter doesn't order the related model at all, even if ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class.
That's because the call to field.get_choices ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L422 omits the ordering kwarg entirely.

Phase payload: Read the problem carefully.
Available tools: python_exec

Thought: <atomic> 
Action: <unparsed>
Observation: <parse_error>:Model output missing required `Action:` line: 'The fix is complete. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Problem

`RelatedFieldListFilter.field_admin_ordering()` returned an empty tuple `()` in two situations:
1. When the related model wasn't registered with any `ModelAdmin` (caught `NotRegistered`)
2. Implicitly when `related_admin.get_ordering(request)` returned `()` (no admin ordering defined)

Neither case fell back to `Model._meta.ordering`, so filter choices appeared unordered.

`RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` already called `field_admin_ordering` and passed the result to `field.get_choices` in the current Django version, so that part of the original bug was already fixed.

## Fix (in `RelatedFieldListFilter.field_admin_ordering`)

```python
def field_admin_ordering(self, field, request, model_admin):
    """
    Return the model admin's ordering for related field, if provided.
    Fall back to the related model's Meta.ordering.
    """
    try:
        related_admin = model_admin.admin_site.get_model_admin(
            field.remote_field.model
        )
    except NotRegistered:
        pass
    else:
        ordering = related_admin.get_ordering(request)
        if ordering:
            return ordering
    return field.remote_field.model._meta.ordering
```

**Priority order:** ModelAdmin ordering → `Meta.ordering` → `()` (empty, if Meta.ordering is also unset)

Both `RelatedFieldListFilter` and `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` inherit this fix since `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` calls `self.field_admin_ordering()` from its `field_choices` method.'
Thought: <atomic> The problem is that `RelatedFieldListFilter` ignores `Model._meta.ordering` when no `ModelAdmin` ordering is defined, and `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` omits the `ordering` kwarg to `field.get_choices`. The target output is a code patch to `django/contrib/admin/filters.py` fixing `field_admin_ordering` to fall back to `Meta.ordering`.
Action: python_exec(code=print('Problem restatement: RelatedFieldListFilter.field_admin_ordering() sets ordering to () when (a) the related model has no ModelAdmin or (b) the ModelAdmin.get_ordering() returns (). Neither case falls back to field.remote_field.model._meta.ordering. RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter omits the ordering kwarg to field.get_choices entirely. Target output: a patch to field_admin_ordering() in django/contrib/admin/filters.py that (1) catches NotRegistered and falls back, and (2) falls back when get_ordering() returns empty, using Meta.ordering as the fallback in both cases.'))
Observation: ERROR: TypeError: _planandsolve_python_exec() got an unexpected keyword argument 'code'
Continue with the next Thought / Action / Confidence block.

Problem: Ordering problem in admin.RelatedFieldListFilter and admin.RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter
Description
	
RelatedFieldListFilter doesn't fall back to the ordering defined in Model._meta.ordering. 
Ordering gets set to an empty tuple in ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L196 and unless ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class it stays an empty tuple. IMHO it should fall back to the ordering defined in the related model's Meta.ordering field.
RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter doesn't order the related model at all, even if ordering is defined on the related model's ModelAdmin class.
That's because the call to field.get_choices ​https://github.com/django/django/blob/2.2.1/django/contrib/admin/filters.py#L422 omits the ordering kwarg entirely.

## Problem

`RelatedFieldListFilter.field_admin_ordering()` returned an empty tuple `()` in two situations:
1. When the related model wasn't registered with any `ModelAdmin` (caught `NotRegistered`)
2. Implicitly when `related_admin.get_ordering(request)` returned `()` (no admin ordering defined)

Neither case fell back to `Model._meta.ordering`, so filter choices appeared unordered.

`RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` already called `field_admin_ordering` and passed the result to `field.get_choices` in the current Django version, so that part of the original bug was already fixed.

## Fix (in `RelatedFieldListFilter.field_admin_ordering`)

```python
def field_admin_ordering(self, field, request, model_admin):
    """
    Return the model admin's ordering for related field, if provided.
    Fall back to the related model's Meta.ordering.
    """
    try:
        related_admin = model_admin.admin_site.get_model_admin(
            field.remote_field.model
        )
    except NotRegistered:
        pass
    else:
        ordering = related_admin.get_ordering(request)
        if ordering:
            return ordering
    return field.remote_field.model._meta.ordering
```

**Priority order:** ModelAdmin ordering → `Meta.ordering` → `()` (empty, if Meta.ordering is also unset)

Both `RelatedFieldListFilter` and `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` inherit this fix since `RelatedOnlyFieldListFilter` calls `self.field_admin_ordering()` from its `field_choices` method.'

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- add-metric: Interactively register one or more project metrics in meta/metrics/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a new measurement needs to be tracked across tasks.
- research-internet: Conduct structured internet research and write `research/research_internet.md`.
- setup-remote-machine: Provision, validate, monitor, and tear down remote GPU machines for task execution. Use when a task needs remote machine setup, monitoring, or teardown.
- self-analyze: Analyze a completed Claude Code or Codex session (task execution, brainstorm, project-description, or any other skill invocation) and identify self-corrected mistakes, uncorrected mistakes that affected the result, and human interventions, then propose concrete ARF framework improvements. Prints findings to the chat; does not save reports.
- add-paper: Download a paper, create metadata assets, and register it in the current task.
- human-brainstorm: Run an interactive brainstorming session to review state and choose next actions.
- add-asset-type: Interactively scaffold a new asset type under meta/asset_types/, grounded in project/description.md. Creates a specification.md stub patterned on the built-in asset types. Use during project setup when a custom asset kind is needed; accept "none for now" to skip.
- research-code: Review libraries and prior tasks to write implementation-oriented code research.
- setup-project: One-shot interactive onboarding for a newly forked Glite ARF template. Shows the safety acknowledgement, installs dependencies, runs doctor.py, chains into create-project-description, provisions the paid services the project declared, then invokes the four meta/ sub-skills. Use once per fork, right after cloning.
- compare-literature: Compare task results against published literature and write the comparison output.
- planning: Synthesize research outputs into a task plan with steps, costs, risks, and checks.
- download_paper: Resolve paper identifiers, fetch the PDF and metadata, and prepare inputs for paper asset creation. Use when you need to download a research paper from partial identifiers.
- generate-suggestions: Generate follow-up task suggestions from completed task outputs and project context.
- research-papers: Review downloaded papers and write `research/research_papers.md`.
- self-improvement: Plan and execute a framework change across every related ARF file — specifications, verificators, aggregators, skills, scripts, hooks, tests, materializers, and docs — in a dedicated infrastructure branch and worktree, with style checks and the full test suite. Use whenever anything under `arf/` or generic parts of `meta/` needs to change.
- add-task-type: Interactively add one or more task types to meta/task_types/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a recurring kind of task needs standardized planning and implementation guidance.
- create-project-description: Guide creation of `project/description.md` and `project/budget.json` for a new or revised ARF project. Use when project goals, scope, or budget need to be defined.
- generate-daily-news: Generate Daily News
- execute-task: Run an ARF task through all required stages and merge the final PR.
- implementation: Execute `plan/plan.md`, produce assets, and verify results.
- create-task: Create a new not-started task folder with task.json and task_description.md. Use when any skill or workflow needs to create a new task.
- check-python-style: Check a Python file against the project Python style guide and report violations. Use after editing Python files or when reviewing code style.
- add-category: Interactively add one or more entries to meta/categories/, grounded in project/description.md. Use during project setup or any time a new cross-cutting tag is needed.
- check-skill: Verify an ARF skill's structure, shared metadata, symlinks, and markdown quality. Use after creating or updating any skill under `arf/skills/`.
- check-markdown-style: Check a Markdown file against the project markdown style guide and report violations. Use after editing `.md` files or when reviewing markdown quality.
- create-dedup-task: Create a deduplication checkpoint task that scans for duplicate papers and overlapping work. Use when the project needs a new dedup checkpoint.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""
2	This encapsulates the logic for displaying filters in the Django admin.
3	Filters are specified in models with the "list_filter" option.
4	
5	Each filter subclass knows how to display a filter for a field that passes a
6	certain test -- e.g. being a DateField or ForeignKey.
7	"""
8	
9	import datetime
10	
11	from django.contrib.admin.exceptions import NotRegistered
12	from django.contrib.admin.options import IncorrectLookupParameters
13	from django.contrib.admin.utils import (
14	    build_q_object_from_lookup_parameters,
15	    get_last_value_from_parameters,
16	    get_model_from_relation,
17	    prepare_lookup_value,
18	    reverse_field_path,
19	)
20	from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured, ValidationError
21	from django.db import models
22	from django.utils import timezone
23	from django.utils.translation import gettext_lazy as _
24	
25	
26	class ListFilter:
27	    title = None  # Human-readable title to appear in the right sidebar.
28	    template = "admin/filter.html"
29	
30	    def __init__(self, request, params, model, model_admin):
31	        self.request = request
32	        # This dictionary will eventually contain the request's query string
33	        # parameters actually used by this filter.
34	        self.used_parameters = {}
35	        if self.title is None:
36	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
37	                "The list filter '%s' does not specify a 'title'."
38	                % self.__class__.__name__
39	            )
40	
41	    def has_output(self):
42	        """
43	        Return True if some choices would be output for this filter.
44	        """
45	        raise NotImplementedError(
46	            "subclasses of ListFilter must provide a has_output() method"
47	        )
48	
49	    def choices(self, changelist):
50	        """
51	        Return choices ready to be output in the template.
52	
53	        `changelist` is the ChangeList to be displayed.
54	        """
55	        raise NotImplementedError(
56	            "subclasses of ListFilter must provide a choices() method"
57	        )
58	
59	    def queryset(self, request, queryset):
60	        """
61	        Return the filtered queryset.
62	        """
63	        raise NotImplementedError(
64	            "subclasses of ListFilter must provide a queryset() method"
65	        )
66	
67	    def expected_parameters(self):
68	        """
69	        Return the list of parameter names that are expected from the
70	        request's query string and that will be used by this filter.
71	        """
72	        raise NotImplementedError(
73	            "subclasses of ListFilter must provide an expected_parameters() method"
74	        )
75	
76	
77	class FacetsMixin:
78	    def get_facet_counts(self, pk_attname, filtered_qs):
79	        raise NotImplementedError(
80	            "subclasses of FacetsMixin must provide a get_facet_counts() method."
81	        )
82	
83	    def get_facet_queryset(self, changelist):
84	        filtered_qs = changelist.get_queryset(
85	            self.request, exclude_parameters=self.expected_parameters()
86	        )
87	        return filtered_qs.aggregate(
88	            **self.get_facet_counts(changelist.pk_attname, filtered_qs)
89	        )
90	
91	
92	class SimpleListFilter(FacetsMixin, ListFilter):
93	    # The parameter that should be used in the query string for that filter.
94	    parameter_name = None
95	
96	    def __init__(self, request, params, model, model_admin):
97	        super().__init__(request, params, model, model_admin)
98	        if self.parameter_name is None:
99	            raise ImproperlyConfigured(
100	                "The list filter '%s' does not specify a 'parameter_name'."
101	                % self.__class__.__name__
102	            )
103	        if self.parameter_name in params:
104	            value = params.pop(self.parameter_name)
105	            self.used_parameters[self.parameter_name] = value[-1]
106	        lookup_choices = self.lookups(request, model_admin)
107	        if lookup_choices is None:
108	            lookup_choices = ()
109	        self.lookup_choices = list(lookup_choices)
110	
111	    def has_output(self):
112	        return len(self.lookup_choices) > 0
113	
114	    def value(self):
115	        """
116	        Return the value (in string format) provided in the request's
117	        query string for this filter, if any, or None if the value wasn't
118	        provided.
119	        """
120	        return self.used_parameters.get(self.parameter_name)
121	
122	    def lookups(self, request, model_admin):
123	        """
124	        Must be overridden to return a list of tuples (value, verbose value)
125	        """
126	        raise NotImplementedError(
127	            "The SimpleListFilter.lookups() method must be overridden to "
128	            "return a list of tuples (value, verbose value)."
129	        )
130	
131	    def expected_parameters(self):
132	        return [self.parameter_name]
133	
134	    def get_facet_counts(self, pk_attname, filtered_qs):
135	        original_value = self.used_parameters.get(self.parameter_name)
136	        counts = {}
137	        for i, choice in enumerate(self.lookup_choices):
138	            self.used_parameters[self.parameter_name] = choice[0]
139	            lookup_qs = self.queryset(self.request, filtered_qs)
140	            if lookup_qs is not None:
141	                counts[f"{i}__c"] = models.Count(
142	                    pk_attname,
143	                    filter=models.Q(pk__in=lookup_qs),
144	                )
145	        self.used_parameters[self.parameter_name] = original_value
146	        return counts
147	
148	    def choices(self, changelist):
149	        add_facets = changelist.add_facets
150	        facet_counts = self.get_facet_queryset(changelist) if add_facets else None
151	        yield {
152	            "selected": self.value() is None,
153	            "query_string": changelist.get_query_string(remove=[self.parameter_name]),
154	            "display": _("All"),
155	        }
156	        for i, (lookup, title) in enumerate(self.lookup_choices):
157	            if add_facets:
158	                if (count := facet_counts.get(f"{i}__c", -1)) != -1:
159	                    title = f"{title} ({count})"
160	                else:
161	                    title = f"{title} (-)"
162	            yield {
163	                "selected": self.value() == str(lookup),
164	                "query_string": changelist.get_query_string(
165	                    {self.parameter_name: lookup}
166	                ),
167	                "display": title,
168	            }
169	
170	
171	class FieldListFilter(FacetsMixin, ListFilter):
172	    _field_list_filters = []
173	    _take_priority_index = 0
174	    list_separator = ","
175	
176	    def __init__(self, field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path):
177	        self.field = field
178	        self.field_path = field_path
179	        self.title = getattr(field, "verbose_name", field_path)
180	        super().__init__(request, params, model, model_admin)
181	        for p in self.expected_parameters():
182	            if p in params:
183	                value = params.pop(p)
184	                self.used_parameters[p] = prepare_lookup_value(
185	                    p, value, self.list_separator
186	                )
187	
188	    def has_output(self):
189	        return True
190	
191	    def queryset(self, request, queryset):
192	        try:
193	            q_object = build_q_object_from_lookup_parameters(self.used_parameters)
194	            return queryset.filter(q_object)
195	        except (ValueError, ValidationError) as e:
196	            # Fields may raise a ValueError or ValidationError when converting
197	            # the parameters to the correct type.
198	            raise IncorrectLookupParameters(e)
199	
200	    @classmethod
201	    def register(cls, test, list_filter_class, take_priority=False):
202	        if take_priority:
203	            # This is to allow overriding the default filters for certain types
204	            # of fields with some custom filters. The first found in the list
205	            # is used in priority.
206	            cls._field_list_filters.insert(
207	                cls._take_priority_index, (test, list_filter_class)
208	            )
209	            cls._take_priority_index += 1
210	        else:
211	            cls._field_list_filters.append((test, list_filter_class))
212	
213	    @classmethod
214	    def create(cls, field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path):
215	        for test, list_filter_class in cls._field_list_filters:
216	            if test(field):
217	                return list_filter_class(
218	                    field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path=field_path
219	                )
220	
221	
222	class RelatedFieldListFilter(FieldListFilter):
223	    def __init__(self, field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path):
224	        other_model = get_model_from_relation(field)
225	        self.lookup_kwarg = "%s__%s__exact" % (field_path, field.target_field.name)
226	        self.lookup_kwarg_isnull = "%s__isnull" % field_path
227	        self.lookup_val = params.get(self.lookup_kwarg)
228	        self.lookup_val_isnull = get_last_value_from_parameters(
229	            params, self.lookup_kwarg_isnull
230	        )
231	        super().__init__(field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path)
232	        self.lookup_choices = self.field_choices(field, request, model_admin)
233	        if hasattr(field, "verbose_name"):
234	            self.lookup_title = field.verbose_name
235	        else:
236	            self.lookup_title = other_model._meta.verbose_name
237	        self.title = self.lookup_title
238	        self.empty_value_display = model_admin.get_empty_value_display()
239	
240	    @property
241	    def include_empty_choice(self):
242	        """
243	        Return True if a "(None)" choice should be included, which filters
244	        out everything except empty relationships.
245	        """
246	        return self.field.null or (self.field.is_relation and self.field.many_to_many)
247	
248	    def has_output(self):
249	        if self.include_empty_choice:
250	            extra = 1

250	            extra = 1
251	        else:
252	            extra = 0
253	        return len(self.lookup_choices) + extra > 1
254	
255	    def expected_parameters(self):
256	        return [self.lookup_kwarg, self.lookup_kwarg_isnull]
257	
258	    def field_admin_ordering(self, field, request, model_admin):
259	        """
260	        Return the model admin's ordering for related field, if provided.
261	        Fall back to the related model's Meta.ordering.
262	        """
263	        try:
264	            related_admin = model_admin.admin_site.get_model_admin(
265	                field.remote_field.model
266	            )
267	        except NotRegistered:
268	            pass
269	        else:
270	            ordering = related_admin.get_ordering(request)
271	            if ordering:
272	                return ordering
273	        return field.remote_field.model._meta.ordering
274	
275	    def field_choices(self, field, request, model_admin):
276	        ordering = self.field_admin_ordering(field, request, model_admin)
277	        return field.get_choices(include_blank=False, ordering=ordering)
278	
279	    def get_facet_counts(self, pk_attname, filtered_qs):

332	class BooleanFieldListFilter(FieldListFilter):
333	    def __init__(self, field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path):
334	        self.lookup_kwarg = "%s__exact" % field_path
335	        self.lookup_kwarg2 = "%s__isnull" % field_path
336	        self.lookup_val = get_last_value_from_parameters(params, self.lookup_kwarg)
337	        self.lookup_val2 = get_last_value_from_parameters(params, self.lookup_kwarg2)
338	        super().__init__(field, request, params, model, model_admin, field_path)
339	        if (
340	            self.used_parameters
341	            and self.lookup_kwarg in self.used_parameters
342	            and self.used_parameters[self.lookup_kwarg] in ("1", "0")
343	        ):
344	            self.used_parameters[self.lookup_kwarg] = bool(
345	                int(self.used_parameters[self.lookup_kwarg])
346	            )
347	
348	    def expected_parameters(self):
349	        return [self.lookup_kwarg, self.lookup_kwarg2]
350	
351	    def get_facet_counts(self, pk_attname, filtered_qs):
352	        return {
353	            "true__c": models.Count(
354	                pk_attname, filter=models.Q(**{self.field_path: True})
355	            ),
356	            "false__c": models.Count(
357	                pk_attname, filter=models.Q(**{self.field_path: False})
358	            ),
359	            "null__c": models.Count(
360	                pk_attname, filter=models.Q(**{self.lookup_kwarg2: True})
361	            ),
362	        }
363	
364	    def choices(self, changelist):
365	        field_choices = dict(self.field.flatchoices)
366	        add_facets = changelist.add_facets
367	        facet_counts = self.get_facet_queryset(changelist) if add_facets else None
368	        for lookup, title, count_field in (
369	            (None, _("All"), None),
370	            ("1", field_choices.get(True, _("Yes")), "true__c"),
371	            ("0", field_choices.get(False, _("No")), "false__c"),
372	        ):
373	            if add_facets:
374	                if count_field is not None:
375	                    count = facet_counts[count_field]
376	                    title = f"{title} ({count})"
377	            yield {
378	                "selected": self.lookup_val == lookup and not self.lookup_val2,
379	                "query_string": changelist.get_query_string(
380	                    {self.lookup_kwarg: lookup}, [self.lookup_kwarg2]
381	                ),
382	                "display": title,
383	            }
384	        if self.field.null:
385	            display = field_choices.get(None, _("Unknown"))
386	            if add_facets:
387	                count = facet_counts["null__c"]
388	                display = f"{display} ({count})"
389	            yield {
390	                "selected": self.lookup_val2 == "True",
391	                "query_string": changelist.get_query_string(
392	                    {self.lookup_kwarg2: "True"}, [self.lookup_kwarg]
393	                ),
394	                "display": display,
395	            }
